questions.q: In which field of science is Meghnad Saha most celebrated?
questions.explanation: Meghnad Saha was an Indian astrophysicist best known for his work on the physics of stars.

questions.q: Meghnad Saha is most famous for which scientific result?
questions.explanation: The Saha ionization equation, which he derived in 1920, bears his name.

questions.q: The Saha equation helped scientists understand the properties of what?
questions.explanation: His equation linked the spectra of stars to their temperature, transforming stellar astrophysics.

questions.q: Meghnad Saha was a scientist of which nationality?
questions.explanation: Saha was an Indian astrophysicist, one of the pioneers of modern science in India.

questions.q: The Saha ionization equation relates the ionization state of a gas to which two physical quantities?
questions.explanation: His equation links the ionisation of a gas to its temperature and pressure (electron density).

questions.q: Saha's work made it possible to determine a key property of stars from their light. What property?
options: Their exact mass in kilograms by eye
questions.explanation: By interpreting stellar spectra, the Saha equation lets astronomers estimate a star's surface temperature.

questions.q: Meghnad Saha studied the splitting of stellar light into a spectrum. This branch of science is called what?
questions.explanation: Spectroscopy is the study of how matter interacts with light, central to Saha's stellar work.

questions.q: Ionization, the key process in Saha's equation, means an atom does what?
options: Gains or loses electrons to become charged
Turns into a different element by decay
questions.explanation: Ionization is the process by which atoms gain or lose electrons and become electrically charged.

questions.q: Which celestial object's spectrum is most directly explained by the Saha equation?
options: A star such as the Sun
questions.explanation: The Saha equation explains how a star's temperature shapes the ionization seen in its spectrum.

questions.q: Meghnad Saha is sometimes described by which nickname reflecting his stellar studies?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as a 'Reader of the Stars' for decoding stellar spectra through his equation.

questions.q: In approximately which year did Saha publish his ionization equation?
questions.explanation: Saha published his theory of thermal ionization around 1920, while working in his late twenties.

questions.q: The Saha equation is essentially an application of which broader area of physics?
questions.explanation: It applies statistical/thermal physics and the idea of chemical equilibrium to the ionization of gases.

questions.q: Saha's equation helped explain why stars of different temperatures show different patterns of what in their spectra?
questions.explanation: It explained the appearance and disappearance of spectral lines as a function of stellar temperature.

questions.q: Saha's theory of thermal ionization provided a physical basis for which classification used by astronomers?
questions.explanation: It gave a physical explanation for the stellar spectral sequence (the O, B, A, F, G, K, M classes).

questions.q: Besides astrophysics, Saha took a strong interest in the planning of which kind of public works in India?
options: River and flood control / hydro projects
questions.explanation: Saha championed river planning and flood control, contributing notably to the Damodar Valley project idea.

questions.q: Meghnad Saha founded a major research institute in Kolkata (Calcutta). Which one?
options: The Institute of Nuclear Physics (later named after him)
questions.explanation: He founded the Institute of Nuclear Physics in Calcutta, later renamed the Saha Institute of Nuclear Physics.

questions.q: Saha's ionization equation is widely used in which present-day discipline beyond stars?
questions.explanation: The equation is fundamental in plasma physics wherever thermal ionization of gases must be calculated.

questions.q: In the later part of his life, Saha entered public life as a member of which body?
questions.explanation: Saha was elected to the Indian Parliament (Lok Sabha) as an independent member in the 1950s.

questions.q: Saha was elected a Fellow of which prestigious British scientific society?
questions.explanation: He was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) in 1927 in recognition of his scientific work.

questions.q: Saha was a contemporary and associate of which other famous Indian physicist, with whom he shared an academic environment in Calcutta?
questions.explanation: Saha and Satyendra Nath Bose were classmates and colleagues, both pioneers of modern physics in India.

questions.q: Saha helped found which long-running Indian science journal?
questions.explanation: He founded the journal 'Science and Culture' to promote scientific thinking and policy in India.

questions.q: Saha played a leading role in reforming which national instrument related to timekeeping in India?
questions.explanation: Saha chaired the committee that produced India's reformed national (Saka) calendar.

questions.q: Saha derived his ionization equation by adapting the dissociation theory of which chemist/physicist to ionization?
questions.explanation: He adapted the thermodynamic dissociation framework (Nernst's thermal/chemical equilibrium approach) to atomic ionization.

questions.q: Saha completed his higher education and early research primarily at which university?
questions.explanation: He studied and began his academic career at the University of Calcutta before later working in Allahabad.

questions.q: After Calcutta, Saha held a long professorship and built a physics department at which university?
questions.explanation: Saha was professor and built up the physics department at the University of Allahabad in the 1920s and 1930s.

questions.q: During his European travels Saha worked in the laboratory of which scientist studying spectra and atomic physics?
options: Alfred Fowler (and later Walther Nernst's circle)
questions.explanation: Saha worked with the spectroscopist Alfred Fowler in London and in Nernst's laboratory in Berlin in the early 1920s.

questions.q: Saha's ionization work is often credited with launching which sub-field of astrophysics?
questions.explanation: His equation is regarded as the foundation of the theory of stellar atmospheres and astrophysical spectroscopy.

questions.q: The institute Saha founded is today a leading Indian centre for research in which area?
questions.explanation: The Saha Institute of Nuclear Physics remains a major centre for nuclear, atomic and condensed-matter physics.

questions.q: In what decade was Meghnad Saha born, in what is now Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Saha was born in 1893 in a village near Dhaka (then in Bengal, now Bangladesh).

questions.q: Saha's equation is frequently compared in importance to the work of which astronomer who applied physics to stellar spectra at Harvard, building on his theory?
questions.explanation: Cecilia Payne-Gaposchkin used Saha's ionization theory to show that stars are made mostly of hydrogen.
